description: Create a global lead generation JavaScript module (lead-gen.js) that implements: (1) Pop-up CTA modal appearing after 10 seconds collecting Nama and WhatsApp number, with 7-day localStorage suppression; (2) Exit-intent popup detecting mouse leaving viewport (desktop) or rapid scroll-up (mobile), with 3-day suppression and awareness of CTA popup state; (3) Floating mobile CTA bar at bottom of screen with contextual WhatsApp link; (4) Enhanced contextual WhatsApp pre-filled messages based on current page URL. Also add corresponding CSS styles for all popup/bar UI elements.
steps: Create public/assets/lead-gen.js with all lead generation logic

Implement the timed popup CTA (10 second delay): Modal with form fields for 'Nama' and 'Nomor WhatsApp', submit redirects to wa.me/6287781896087 with pre-filled message containing user data, close button, localStorage check with key 'pc_popup_cta_shown' storing timestamp, 7-day expiry logic

Implement exit-intent popup: Desktop detection via mouseout event on document (clientY < 0), Mobile detection via scroll direction change (rapid upward scroll), different compelling copy ('Tunggu! Konsultasi GRATIS sebelum Anda pergi'), same form fields (Nama, WhatsApp), localStorage key 'pc_exit_intent_shown' with 3-day expiry, skip if popup CTA was already shown/submitted in this session

Implement floating mobile CTA bar: Fixed position bottom bar visible only on mobile (max-width: 768px), WhatsApp icon + text 'Chat Sekarang - Konsultasi Gratis', contextual pre-filled message based on current page path using a mapping object

Implement contextual WhatsApp message enhancement: Create a PAGE_MESSAGES mapping object that maps page URLs to specific Indonesian messages (e.g., /pages/pap.html -> 'Halo, saya tertarik dengan produk PAP (Proteksi Asuransi Prima). Bisa dibantu?', /pages/mdsa.html -> 'Halo, saya tertarik dengan produk MDSA. Bisa konsultasi?', etc. for all pages). On page load, update all existing wa.me links on the page to use contextual messages

Add CSS styles either inline in lead-gen.js or as a dedicated section: Style the modal overlay (dark backdrop), modal box (centered, max-width 420px, white bg with dark mode support), form inputs (matching existing .calc-select-group style), submit button (btn--cta style), floating bar (fixed bottom, z-index high, jade/green background, white text), close buttons, animations (fadeIn/slideUp)

Include the script in the page load flow: Add lead-gen.js loading in public/assets/main.js init() function after components are loaded, OR add a script tag reference. The script should self-initialize when DOM is ready.
acceptance_criteria: public/assets/lead-gen.js exists and contains all popup, exit-intent, floating bar, and contextual message logic
Pop-up CTA stores timestamp in localStorage under 'pc_popup_cta_shown' and respects 7-day cooldown
Exit-intent popup stores timestamp under 'pc_exit_intent_shown' and respects 3-day cooldown
Exit-intent does not show if pop-up CTA was already triggered in the same session
Floating CTA bar only shows on viewports <= 768px
WhatsApp links on product pages get updated with contextual messages based on page URL
All form submissions redirect to https://wa.me/6287781896087 with properly URL-encoded message containing user name and number
All UI elements support dark mode (html.dark class)
The script is referenced/loaded from index.html and all pages (via the main.js init or a new script tag in component footer)
verification: Read public/assets/lead-gen.js and confirm it contains popup logic, exit-intent logic, floating bar logic, and contextual message mapping
Confirm the script is loaded via main.js or referenced in footer.html component
Verify localStorage key names and expiry logic are correct
Check that the WhatsApp URL format uses 6287781896087 as the number
findings: Implementation complete. lead-gen.js is loaded dynamically from main.js init() function using document.createElement('script'). All CSS is injected via JS style element. Node --check confirms no syntax errors in both files. The script self-initializes when DOM is ready.
